channel.title: Upcoming events on Todmorden Events
channel.description: Recent content in Upcoming events on Todmorden Events

item.title: Test Title 6
item.description: TESt/

item.title: Art Therapy
item.description: A safe, gentle healing creative group for people who have experienced life challenges, such as (but not exclusively) trauma, anxiety or depression.

item.title: Pickleball
item.description: Weekly sessions of Pickleball. Please contact Angela before attending. (This class is currently full, if any spots become available, please contact Angela and she will let you know).

item.title: Andys Man Club
item.description:
Andys Man Club offer a free to attend peer-to-peer support group every Monday, except bank holidays. In these groups, men can open up about the storms affecting their lives in a safe, judgement-free and non-clinical environment. These clubs are designed to be free of pressure, there is no obligation for men to speak, they can simply listen if they wish. We know it can be daunting contemplating coming to a club with people you don’t know, but don’t be afraid to come along and see for yourself. It might be the best decision you make. Please remember this group is 18+

ANDYSMANCLUB are a men’s suicide prevention charity that aims to end the stigma surrounding men’s mental health and help men through the power of conversation. #ITSOKAYTOTALK

item.title: Andys Man Club
item.description:
Andys Man Club offer a free to attend peer-to-peer support group every Monday, except bank holidays. In these groups, men can open up about the storms affecting their lives in a safe, judgement-free and non-clinical environment. These clubs are designed to be free of pressure, there is no obligation for men to speak, they can simply listen if they wish. We know it can be daunting contemplating coming to a club with people you don’t know, but don’t be afraid to come along and see for yourself. It might be the best decision you make. Please remember this group is 18+

ANDYSMANCLUB are a men’s suicide prevention charity that aims to end the stigma surrounding men’s mental health and help men through the power of conversation. #ITSOKAYTOTALK

item.title: Todmorden Karate Club
item.description: Traditional Shotokan Karate, Taught by 7th Dan Black Belt , Bob Smith.

item.title: Kixx Football - For children aged 3-8
item.description:
Kixx is a children’s football academy, who provide a high-energy, fun, and imaginative introduction to physical activity, all through the power of football.

Kixx sessions are also a brilliant way for children to build friendships and grow in confidence. Every class is carefully designed for both boys and girls aged 18 months to 10 years, ensuring the activities are age-appropriate, inclusive, and engaging.

The Kixx curriculum focuses on developing both physical skills and social skills — helping every child learn, move, and thrive in a supportive environment.

Our vision is simple: to inspire every child to be better through football.

Or you can directly contact Coach Cam – camerontimberley@kixx.org.uk

item.title: Banking Hub
item.description:
Our Banking Hub, provided by Cash Access, operates Monday – Friday. The Banking Hub has a counter where you can access cash services whenever the hub is open and whoever you bank with:

pay in cash (notes and coins) and cheques

withdraw cash (notes and coins)

check your balance

pay utility bills

top up your gas and electricity

access change-giving services (for registered businesses) 

You can also talk to a community banker from your bank on the day your bank is in the hub. There is a private space where you can talk face-to-face about more complicated banking enquiries like payments and transfers, managing your account, and how to use online banking. You can view the services available from community bankers from the Community Banker Services page on the Cash Access website.

Monday – Virgin Money

Tuesday – Lloyds

Wednesday – Halifax

Thursday – Natwest

Friday – Barclays

item.title: Test Title 4
item.description: TESt/

item.title: Test 2 b
item.description: test

item.title: Test 2
item.description: test

item.title: Family Coding Club
item.description:
An informal drop-in coding session for children and families. Activities include simple games, creative coding, and playful experiments with computers.

No prior experience needed. Suitable for ages 7+ with an accompanying adult.

item.title: Game jam taster, build a tiny game in two hours
item.description:
A relaxed introduction to game jams. We will form small teams (or work solo), pick a playful theme, and build a tiny game using a starter template.

No experience needed. Bring a laptop if you can.

item.title: Monday Website Club - Feb
item.description: A test event

item.title: test1
item.description: test 3

item.title: Repair Café
item.description:
A regular Repair Café where volunteers help fix household items such as small appliances, clothing, and bikes. Bring along something broken and see if it can be repaired rather than thrown away.

No charge, donations welcome.

item.title: Pixel art for games, characters and tiles
item.description:
A practical session focused on making simple sprites, character animations, and tiles for top-down and platform games.

Beginners welcome. Suitable for ages 9+ with an accompanying adult.

item.title: Side missions and mini quests workshop
item.description:
A short, guided workshop on designing side missions and mini quests to add choice, challenge, and humour to your game.

Bring a laptop if you can. We will have a small number available to borrow.

item.title: JAMM Labs game making drop-in
item.description:
An informal drop-in for children, young people, and families to explore game making using simple templates, pixel art, and playful experiments.

Suitable for ages 8+ with an accompanying adult. No prior coding experience needed.

item.title: JAMM Labs game making drop-in
item.description:
An informal drop-in for children, young people, and families to explore game making using simple templates, pixel art, and playful experiments.

Suitable for ages 8+ with an accompanying adult. No prior coding experience needed.
